original_abstract: Cyclic molecular scaffolds, particularly benzannulated medium-sized and macrocyclic ring (BMR) systems, represent a cornerstone of organic chemistry. These structures are integral components of a vast array of biologically significant molecules, ranging from intricate natural products to potent synthetic pharmaceuticals.
humanized_abstract: Cyclic molecular frameworks, a.k.a. benzannulated medium-sized and macrocyclic ring (BMR) systems, are a fundamental unit of organic chemistry and are part of many significant bioactive molecules such as complex natural products and synthetically derived small molecule pharmaceuticals.

document.output: Cyclic molecular frameworks, a.k.a. benzannulated medium-sized and macrocyclic ring (BMR) systems, are a fundamental unit of organic chemistry and are part of many significant bioactive molecules such as complex natural products and synthetically derived small molecule pharmaceuticals.
